name: Pediatric Appendicitis Score
purpose: Diagnosis and rule out of appendicitis in pediatric patients.
when_to_use: Children or adolescents with acute abdominal pain, especially localized to the right lower quadrant (RLQ), where appendicitis is suspected

items.label: rlq_tenderness_cough_percussion_hopping
items.description: Right lower quadrant (RLQ) tenderness to cough, percussion, or hopping

items.label: migration_of_pain_to_rlq
items.description: Migration of pain to right lower quadrant

formula: rlq_tenderness_cough_percussion_hopping + anorexia + fever + nausea_or_vomiting + tenderness_right_iliac_fossa + leukocytosis + neutrophilia + migration_of_pain_to_rlq

interpretation.description: Low Risk: Low likelihood of acute appendicitis, and likely do not warrant imaging. Consider other causes of acute abdominal pain.
Equivocal: Imaging can be helpful, preferably ultrasound or MRI. Surgical consults are warranted for patients with equivocal scores and imaging where the appendix cannot be visualized.
High Risk: Surgical consult warranted. Imaging may still be pursued, but patients should only undergo ultrasound prior to a surgical consult.

references.citation: Shah SR, Sinclair KA, Theut SB, Johnson KM, Holcomb GW 3rd, St Peter SD. Computed Tomography Utilization for the Diagnosis of Acute Appendicitis in Children Decreases With a Diagnostic Algorithm. Ann Surg. 2016 Sep;264(3):474-81.

notes: Patients in the low risk group according to the PAS do not have a zero risk. Use clinical discretion if imaging or surgical consult may aid in diagnosis. The score has a higher negative predictive value (95%) in the absence of RLQ pain, pain with walking/jumping or coughing, and an ANC <6,750. For patients who are not low risk, next steps include NPO status, IV fluids, analgesia, and imaging or surgical consultation.
